line: - Registered in MCP Agent Mail as `IndigoStream`.
- Checked active agents and sent a coordination note on `mb-2db.8`.

- Confirmed the real remaining gap: local CLI recall/explain/budget used a separate in-process retrieval path and did not support opt-in mood-congruent ranking, even though daemon/runtime already did.
What I changed in the CLI slice:
- Preserved affect on CLI-local encode/observe records.
- Added local CLI helpers mirroring daemon affect logic:
- Threaded the opt-in flag through local recall/explain/budget result building.
- I could not complete compilation/test validation because the workspace already fails earlier on an unrelated upstream error:
- `error[E0063]: missing field compression in initializer of NormalizedMemoryEnvelope`
So I stopped short of touching that overlapping core issue.
- The missing F18 slice was not just a CLI flag; the CLI had its own retrieval path that needed real local affect-aware ranking support.

items: The missing F18 slice was not just a CLI flag; the CLI had its own retrieval path that needed real local affect-aware ranking support.
